title: Porous mediums for capture and detection of gases
abstract: A porous medium for capturing gases comprising a metal complex of the formula: L âM âX 2 R, wherein: L is an aromatic cyclic ligand; M is a group 8 or 9 transition metal; and X 2 R is an unsaturated bidentate ligand wherein X is S, Se or Te or a combination thereof; and wherein the metal complex is an electron deficient complex. A method for forming the same, a method of recycling the porous medium containing a captured gas and uses of the porous medium.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A porous medium for capturing gases comprising a metal complex of the formula: L - M - X 2 R wherein: L is an aromatic cyclic ligand; M is a group 8 or 9 transition metal; and X 2 R is an unsaturated bidentate ligand wherein X is S, Se or Te or a combination thereof; and wherein the metal complex is an electron deficient complex. 2. The porous medium of claim 1 wherein the medium is a substrate impregnated with the metal complex. 3. The porous medium of claim 2 wherein the substrate is a gel, polymer or sponge. 4. The porous medium of claim 3 wherein the gel is derived from polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) or a hydrogel. 5. The porous medium of any one of the preceding claims wherein the porous medium is recyclable. 6. The porous medium of any of the preceding claims wherein the aromatic cyclic ligand is benzene, cyclopentadiene or a derivative thereof. 7. The porous medium of any of the preceding claims wherein the group 8 or 9 transition metal is iron, ruthenium, osmium, cobalt, rhodium or iridium. 8. The porous medium of any of the preceding claims wherein the unsaturated bidentate ligand is a bulky ligand. 9. The porous medium of any of the preceding claims wherein the unsaturated bidentate ligand comprises dodecarborane or a derivative thereof. 10. The porous medium of any of preceding claims wherein the unsaturated bidentate ligand is a dithiolene ligand. 11. A method of forming the porous medium of any of claims 1 to 10 comprising the steps of: - providing the porous medium; and - distributing the metal complex in the porous medium. 12. The method of claim 11 wherein the porous medium is a gel or polymer and the metal complex is encapsulated in the gel or polymer to form a metal complex-doped gel or polymer. 13. The method of claim 11 or claim 12 wherein the metal complex is mixed with a gel or polymer and a cross-linker to form the metal complex-doped gel or polymer. 14. The method of any one of claims 11 to 13 wherein the gel or polymer is subsequently heated. 15. The method of claim 11 wherein the porous medium comprises a sponge impregnated with the metal complex. 16. A method for recycling a porous medium, wherein the porous medium is the porous medium of any of claims 1 to 10 containing a captured gas, said method comprising removing the captured gas to provide a recycled porous medium. 17. The method of claim 16 wherein removing the captured gas comprising one or more of exposing the porous medium to an organic solvent, blue light or heat. 18. The method of claim 17 wherein the organic solvent is a non-polar solvent. 19. The method of claim 16 to claim 18 further comprising exposing the recycled porous medium to a gas to capture and/or detect the gas. 20. Use of at least one porous medium comprising a metal complex of any of claims 1 to 10 for the detection of a toxic gas. 21. The use of claim 20 wherein the porous medium is a gel is derived from polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS), a hydrogel, a polymer or a sponge. 22. The use of claim 20 or claim 21 wherein the metal complex comprises a dodecarborane or a derivative thereof. 23. The use of any of claims 20 to 22 wherein two or more porous mediums are used, wherein each porous medium is different. 24. Use of the porous medium comprising a metal complex of any of claims 1 to 10 as a filter for fuel cells. 25. The use of claim 24 wherein the fuel cells are hydrogen fuel cells. 26. Use of the porous medium comprising a metal complex of any of claims 1 to 10 for the purification or separation of gases. 27. Use of the porous medium comprising a metal complex of any of claims 1 to 10 for the storage and/or transportation of toxic gases. 28. Use of the porous medium comprising a metal complex of any of claims 1 to 10 for on demand release of a toxic gas. 29. The use of claim 20, claim 27 or claim 28 wherein the toxic gas is carbon monoxide, phosphorus, sulphur, nitric oxide or cyanide. 30. The use of any of claims 20 to 29, wherein the porous medium is recyclable.
